OTAZKA: A company has a Primary Management Server and one Secondary Management Server. An administrator publishes a large set of object changes on the Primary and then immediately checks the Secondary. Which statement best describes what should and should not synchronize in a normal Management HA state?
A: Published changes synchronize to the peer, while unpublished sessions do not synchronize
B: Only unpublished sessions synchronize immediately because they are still in the administrator database
C: Both published and unpublished changes synchronize because the peer must be identical at all times
D: Only policy packages synchronize, while objects and administrator sessions never synchronize
Vysvetleni: Management HA synchronization is tied to published database changes. Automatic synchronization is initiated after a session on the Active Management Server is published. Unpublished sessions are intentionally not synchronized, which is a common exam trap because SmartConsole may show local session changes before they are part of the shared database.

OTAZKA: During a maintenance window, the Active Management Server is shut down unexpectedly. The Standby Management Server is healthy and fully synchronized. What is the correct operational expectation for failover?
A: The Standby becomes Active automatically after missing three synchronization intervals
B: The Security Gateways elect the Standby Management Server as Active automatically
C: Failover occurs only after cpstart is run on every managed Gateway
D: An administrator must manually perform a changeover from SmartConsole
Vysvetleni: Management HA does not perform automatic failover. A Standby server is ready to take over, but the changeover is a manual administrator action through the Management High Availability menu. This differs from traffic cluster failover and is therefore a frequent trick question.

OTAZKA: A Standby Management Server is changed to Active while it cannot communicate with the original Active server. Later the original server comes back online and still believes it is Active. What state has been created?
A: Split policy mode, where Gateways select the newest policy from both servers
B: Read-only mode, where both servers become Standby until SIC is reset
C: Collision mode, where more than one Management Server is Active and synchronization stops
D: Distributed log mode, where only logging is affected and management remains normal
Vysvetleni: This is Collision Mode. In a normal Management HA deployment only one server should be Active. If two servers are Active, they do not synchronize. The situation must be manually corrected, usually by choosing which server should remain Active and synchronizing the peer.

OTAZKA: An administrator wants to force a Management HA synchronization because the peer was offline during earlier published changes. What is the main difference between manual synchronization and normal automatic synchronization?
A: Manual synchronization only sends changed objects and never policy packages
B: Manual synchronization can synchronize unpublished sessions if the administrator is logged in
C: Manual synchronization performs a full synchronization on demand
D: Manual synchronization changes the synchronization interval for future updates
Vysvetleni: Automatic synchronization can be incremental after the initial full sync. Manual synchronization is used on demand and supports full synchronization. It does not change the synchronization settings and it does not include unpublished sessions.

OTAZKA: While creating the Check Point Host object for a Secondary Management Server, the administrator establishes SIC before enabling Network Policy Management. The peer does not synchronize correctly. What is the likely corrective action?
A: Reset SIC on the Secondary Management Server and establish SIC again after enabling Network Policy Management
B: Enable Identity Awareness because all Management HA peers require identity sharing
C: Run fw fetch localhost on the Secondary Management Server
D: Convert the Secondary Management Server into a Log Server object and then back to a host
Vysvetleni: The course workflow stresses that Network Policy Management must be enabled before completing the communication step. If it is not enabled before the next step, SIC must be reset on the Secondary and re-established so the servers can synchronize correctly.

OTAZKA: After adding a Secondary Management Server object and successful initial synchronization, Gateways still fetch policy only from the original Primary. What configuration step was most likely missed?
A: Adding the Secondary Management Server to the Fetch Policy list on the Gateway or Cluster object
B: Adding the Secondary Management Server to the VPN community as a center Gateway
C: Running migrate_server import on the Gateway
D: Disabling automatic synchronization in Global Properties
Vysvetleni: Management HA synchronization of the management database is not the same as Gateway reconfiguration. The Gateway or Cluster object must be updated so it can fetch policy from the Secondary Management Server. Log settings and database or policy installation are also part of the workflow.

OTAZKA: A Secondary Management Server is synchronized and visible in SmartConsole. An administrator connects directly to it before a changeover. What behavior should be expected in a healthy normal state?
A: The administrator gets full read and write access because the database is synchronized
B: The session is Read Only because the Secondary is Standby
C: The session is blocked unless the Primary is unreachable
D: The Secondary automatically asks whether it should become Active
Vysvetleni: Standby Management Servers operate passively and in Read Only mode. They can be inspected, but management changes are performed on the Active server unless a manual changeover is done and the administrator reconnects to the new Active server.

OTAZKA: A new SmartConsole connection to the Secondary Management Server shows a fingerprint that differs from the Primary Management Server fingerprint. Which interpretation is correct?
A: This is expected because each Management Server has its own fingerprint
B: This proves that the Secondary was not synchronized
C: This means the Secondary must use the same SIC activation key as the Primary forever
D: This indicates that the Secondary is in Collision Mode
Vysvetleni: The fingerprint identifies the specific server that SmartConsole is connecting to. Different Management Servers have different fingerprints. The course lab even asks the administrator to notice that the Secondary has a different fingerprint from the Primary.

OTAZKA: In the Management HA deployment workflow, which sequence best matches the high-level order expected by the course material?
A: Configure Gateways first, test failover second, deploy the Secondary last
B: Install policy first, publish changes second, create the Secondary object third
C: Deploy the Secondary Management Server, configure Gateway or Cluster objects, then verify the Management HA solution
D: Create a VPN community, import a database, then run First Time Configuration Wizard
Vysvetleni: The high-level workflow is divided into three parts. First deploy the Secondary Management Server. Second configure the Security Gateway or Cluster object. Third verify the Management HA solution. Many individual tasks exist inside those parts, but this is the exam-level structure.

OTAZKA: In a failover test, the original Active Management Server has Check Point processes stopped with cpstop. The Secondary is set Active and reports that changeover succeeded with errors because the peer was not switched to Standby. What is the real risk when cpstart is later run on the original server?
A: The original server deletes all synchronized policies because cpstop clears the database
B: The original server may return as Active, producing an Active-Active collision state
C: The Secondary automatically formats its logs to avoid duplicate log IDs
D: The Security Gateways automatically switch to local policy only
Vysvetleni: Because the original Active could not be contacted during the changeover, it was not changed to Standby. When its Check Point processes start again, it can still be Active. That creates Collision Mode and requires manual recovery and synchronization.

OTAZKA: A synchronized Secondary Management Server was just set to Active. SmartConsole displays a message that the connected server is now active and asks the administrator to reconnect. Why is reconnecting important?
A: Reconnect is required only when NAT is configured on the Management Server
B: Reconnect forces all Security Gateways to fetch policy from localhost
C: Reconnect deletes the old peer certificate
D: The current connection remains in read-only mode until a new session is opened
Vysvetleni: When the connected server changes role, the existing SmartConsole session does not automatically become a normal read and write session. Reconnecting to the now Active Management Server is required to work in the correct mode.

OTAZKA: Which statement about Management HA synchronization settings is the most accurate?
A: The administrator can tune the interval and switch between incremental and full modes in Global Properties
B: Synchronization settings are preconfigured and cannot be changed by the administrator
C: Synchronization is configured per Gateway object under Fetch Policy
D: Synchronization works only if the Secondary is also a dedicated Log Server
Vysvetleni: The course notes explicitly state that synchronization settings are preconfigured and cannot be changed by the administrator. Gateway Fetch Policy settings are related to where Gateways can fetch policy from, not to the HA database synchronization interval.

OTAZKA: SmartConsole initiates a policy installation after several changes. Which component makes the decision between a Fast accelerated install and a Full policy install?
A: CPD on the Security Gateway
B: The SmartEvent Correlation Unit
C: CPTA on the Security Gateway
D: CPM on the Security Management Server
Vysvetleni: The policy installation process starts when CPM receives the install command. CPM decides whether to use a Fast accelerated install or a Full install based on the changes made since the last installation. CPD and CPTA participate later on the Gateway side.

OTAZKA: A policy installation uses a Modern Dump. Which statement correctly distinguishes Modern Dump from Legacy Dump?
A: Modern Dump always forces a Full policy installation
B: Modern Dump is stored only on the Gateway and never on the Management Server
C: Modern Dump contains pre-generated code and does not require the FWM verification and conversion stage
D: Modern Dump is used only for NAT rules and cannot include Access Control objects
Vysvetleni: With R81 and higher, Modern Dump contains pre-generated code that does not need further compilation or verification before transfer. Legacy Dump files go through FWM verification and conversion. Mixing these stages is a classic installation-flow trap.

OTAZKA: During the Transfer phase of policy installation, where does the Gateway initially store the transferred policy files before they are committed?
A: $FWDIR/state/local/FW1
B: $FWDIR/state/<Gateway Name>/FW1
C: $CPDIR/conf/FW1
D: $FWDIR/state/__tmp/FW1
Vysvetleni: CPTA transfers policy files to the Security Gateway and CPD stores them temporarily in $FWDIR/state/__tmp/FW1. After successful commit, they are moved to the local storage directory $FWDIR/state/local/FW1.

OTAZKA: After a successful policy installation, where are the policy files located on the Security Gateway and on the Security Management Server?
A: Both Gateway and Management in $FWDIR/state/__tmp/FW1
B: Gateway in $FWDIR/state/<Gateway Name>/FW1 and Management in $FWDIR/state/local/FW1
C: Gateway in $FWDIR/state/local/FW1 and Management in $FWDIR/state/<Gateway Name>/FW1
D: Gateway in $CPDIR/tmp and Management in $CPDIR/database
Vysvetleni: The final local policy location on the Gateway is $FWDIR/state/local/FW1. On the Management Server, compiled files for the target are stored under $FWDIR/state/<Gateway Name>/FW1. The temporary __tmp path is only part of the transfer process.

OTAZKA: An administrator has made Access Control changes in SmartConsole but has not published the session. They start an Install Policy operation. What is the expected result?
A: The unpublished changes are included because installation compiles the working session
B: The unpublished changes are not included in the policy installation
C: Only changed services are included, but changed network objects are excluded
D: The installation fails because unpublished changes always block installation
Vysvetleni: Policy installation uses the published database state. Unpublished administrator session changes are not included. This mirrors the Management HA rule that unpublished sessions do not synchronize.

OTAZKA: A rule must match users from Active Directory without enabling Identity Awareness. Which object type from the course is the best fit, assuming the required Account Unit exists?
A: LDAP Group object
B: Access Role object
C: Network Feed object
D: Dynamic Object
Vysvetleni: LDAP Group objects do not require Identity Awareness, but they do require an LDAP Account Unit with credentials to access the directory. Access Role objects are the more modern approach, but they require Identity Awareness.

OTAZKA: A policy must refer to a resource named FileServer, but the actual IP address differs on every Security Gateway. Which object concept is intended for this problem?
A: Updatable Object imported from Check Point Cloud
B: Network Feed with a fixed HTTPS URL
C: Access Role with Any user and Any machine
D: Dynamic Object resolved locally on each Security Gateway
Vysvetleni: Dynamic Objects have a name in SmartConsole but no fixed IP address in the management database. They are used in policy and resolved locally on each Security Gateway, where they are managed with the dynamic_objects command in Expert mode.

OTAZKA: An administrator imports an Office 365 service object into a rule and later tries to edit the underlying IP list manually. Why is this approach wrong?
A: Office 365 can only be used in NAT rules and not in Access Control
B: Updatable Objects are predefined and cannot be edited manually in SmartConsole
C: The object becomes editable only after a policy installation
D: The object must be converted into a Dynamic Object before use
Vysvetleni: Updatable Objects represent external services whose IP and domain data is maintained and updated dynamically. They are imported into rules, but the predefined object content is not manually edited by the administrator.

OTAZKA: A team wants to use an externally hosted list of IP addresses in Access Control without editing and installing policy every time the list changes. Which feature best matches this need?
A: User Template object with expiration
B: Network Feed object fetched by Security Gateways
C: Manual static NAT rule with Proxy ARP
D: SmartEvent Offline Job
Vysvetleni: Network Feed objects let Security Gateways fetch data from an external HTTP or HTTPS source. They can be used in policy so the external list can change without manual object editing and policy installation for each list update.

OTAZKA: When creating a Network Feed object, which combination is most accurate based on the course material?
A: HTTPS is recommended, flat list or JSON parsing can be configured, and the default feed update interval is 60 minutes
B: Only FTP URLs are supported, JSON is mandatory, and updates happen only after policy install
C: The feed is downloaded by SmartConsole only and then embedded permanently in policy
D: The feed must contain only user names because it is an Identity Awareness object
Vysvetleni: The course describes Network Feeds as external HTTP or HTTPS sources, with HTTPS recommended. Feed parsing can be flat list or JSON. The default check feed interval is 60 minutes and the Gateway is the component that connects to the feed.

OTAZKA: A Network Feed test fails during object configuration. What does the Test Feed action primarily validate?
A: That all Security Gateways have the same Jumbo Hotfix
B: That the feed is already present in the SmartEvent database
C: That the Gateway can connect to the feed URL and validate the certificate of the server containing the feed URL
D: That the feed can be used as an LDAP Account Unit
Vysvetleni: The Test Feed action verifies connectivity from the Gateway to the external feed URL and validates the certificate of the server hosting the feed. It is not a policy-install verification and it is not related to SmartEvent or LDAP.

OTAZKA: A Manual Static NAT rule translates a server to an address from the same network to which the Security Gateway is directly connected. What extra configuration is required?
A: A migrate_server export of the NAT rulebase
B: A VPN community containing the translated address
C: A SmartEvent Correlation Unit for the translated address
D: Proxy ARP on the individual Security Gateway, followed by Security Policy installation
Vysvetleni: Proxy ARP is required for Manual NAT when the translated address belongs to a network where the Gateway is connected. It is configured on the individual Gateway using Gaia Portal, and Security Policy must be installed after every Proxy ARP change.

OTAZKA: A translated NAT address is not from a directly connected network, but from a network routed to the Security Gateway. Which statement is correct?
A: No Proxy ARP is needed for that translated address
B: Proxy ARP is still mandatory for every Manual NAT rule
C: The NAT rule must be automatic, not manual
D: The address must be configured as an Updatable Object
Vysvetleni: The course states that Proxy ARP is required for Manual NAT when using IP addresses from a network where the Gateway is connected. If the translated address is from a network routed to the Gateway, no Proxy ARP is needed.

OTAZKA: Which scenario is a strong reason to use Manual NAT instead of relying only on Automatic NAT?
A: You need to synchronize Management HA peers
B: You need to translate destination ports or translate both source and destination in the same packet
C: You need to make a user group from local administrators
D: You need to update the Deployment Agent
Vysvetleni: Manual NAT gives granular control, including conditional source or destination matching, translating both source and destination in one packet, one-way static NAT, service or destination-port translation, port-specific rules, and NAT for Dynamic Objects.

OTAZKA: An administrator adds Manual Proxy ARP entries in Gaia Portal but does not enable Merge manual proxy ARP configuration. What is the missing SmartConsole-side setting?
A: Global Properties, NAT, Merge manual proxy ARP configuration
B: Gateway object, VPN Domain, Exclude manual ARP
C: SmartEvent Policy, General Settings, Correlation Units
D: CPUSE, Package Repository, Automatic
Vysvetleni: The manual NAT workflow includes configuring Proxy ARP on the Gateway, then enabling Merge manual proxy ARP configuration in SmartConsole Global Properties under NAT. The Security Policy must then be installed for the changes to take effect.

OTAZKA: In R82 Security Management behind NAT, which settings are configured on the Management Server object rather than on the Gateway or Cluster object?
A: Values for Address Translation, Automatic NAT rules, Connections from Security Gateways to this server, and Apply to Security Gateway Control Connections
B: Pending Gateways, gClish, Sync interface and SMO
C: VPN Routing, Tunnel Management, Shared Secret and IKE Phase II
D: Offline Jobs, Correlation Units, Internal Network and Automatic Reactions
Vysvetleni: The course splits Security Management behind NAT settings between the Management Server and the Gateway or Cluster. On the Management Server side you configure address translation values, automatic NAT rule behavior, how Gateways connect to this server, and whether to apply NAT to Gateway control connections.

OTAZKA: A Security Management Server behind NAT is configured to make Gateways use the translated IP address of the server. Which extra condition from the R82 feature is easy to miss?
A: IKEv2 must be disabled globally
B: Identity Awareness must be enabled on every Gateway
C: The Security Management Server must be a Dynamic Object
D: The Management interface must also be configured on the Gateway
Vysvetleni: The option to use the server translated IP address is an R82 plus Gateway feature and also requires configuration of the Management interface on the Gateway. This is separate from creating automatic NAT rules.

OTAZKA: What happens if Apply to Security Gateway Control Connections is disabled for Security Management behind NAT?
A: All NAT rules become manual only
B: Remote Gateways do not communicate with the NAT address of the Management Server
C: The Management Server stops generating audit logs
D: The Gateway ignores policy installation from both original and translated addresses
Vysvetleni: The course note says that if Apply to Security Gateway Control Connections is disabled, a remote Gateway does not communicate with the NAT address of the Management Server. This setting is specifically about control connections, not data-plane NAT for protected servers.

OTAZKA: For Security Management behind NAT on R82 plus Gateways, what is the effect of selecting Do not create automatic NAT rules on the Management Server NAT settings?
A: All Manual NAT rules are deleted
B: Automatic NAT rules are ignored or overridden
C: Only Hide NAT remains active and Static NAT is disabled
D: Proxy ARP is enabled automatically on all Gateways
Vysvetleni: The course identifies Do not create automatic NAT rules as an R82 plus Gateway option. When it is enabled, automatic NAT rules are ignored or overridden. It does not create Proxy ARP and it does not delete manual rules.

OTAZKA: A site-to-site VPN is successfully formed between two Check Point Gateways. Which set of security properties does the IPSec VPN tunnel guarantee according to the course?
A: Identity Awareness, URL filtering and anti-bot
B: Availability, logging and compliance
C: Confidentiality, integrity and authenticity
D: NAT, routing and policy installation
Vysvetleni: The IPSec VPN solution encrypts and decrypts traffic and the tunnel guarantees confidentiality through encryption, integrity through standard integrity methods, and authenticity through standard authentication methods.

OTAZKA: What is the role of IKE in Check Point IPSec VPN tunnel establishment?
A: It installs Firewall policy on the Gateway
B: It updates Office 365 Updatable Objects
C: It authenticates peers, agrees on keys and encryption methods, and produces symmetric keys
D: It performs Management HA database synchronization
Vysvetleni: IKE is the key management protocol used to create VPN tunnels. It authenticates peers, negotiates keys and methods, and creates symmetric key material used for encryption and decryption.

OTAZKA: Which statement correctly compares IKEv1 and IKEv2 in the course material?
A: IKEv2 is required whenever Pre-Shared Secret authentication is used
B: IKEv2 is the default for all older systems, while IKEv1 is used only for IPv6
C: IKEv1 and IKEv2 both use the same Phase I and Phase II packet structure
D: IKEv1 is the default and broadly supported, while IKEv2 is newer and supports IPv6, but Check Point Remote Access VPN clients do not currently support it
Vysvetleni: The course describes IKEv1 as the default version supported on most new and older systems. IKEv2 is newer and supports IPv6, but Check Point Remote Access VPN clients do not currently support it. IKEv2 does not use the same two-phase concept as IKEv1.

OTAZKA: A VPN log indicates IKEv1 Phase I completion. Which result and default lifetime should you associate with Phase I?
A: Two unidirectional IPSec SAs with a default validity of one hour
B: A policy package stored in $FWDIR/state/local/FW1
C: A bidirectional IKE SA with a default validity of one day
D: A Network Feed object with a default interval of 60 minutes
Vysvetleni: IKEv1 Phase I secures the IKE communication itself. The result is a bidirectional IKE Security Association with a default validity of one day. Phase II produces the two unidirectional IPSec SAs with a default validity of one hour.

OTAZKA: A third-party peer supports only Aggressive Mode. What is the best interpretation of using Aggressive Mode in IKEv1 Phase I?
A: It is preferred and default because it uses fewer packets
B: It is the only mode that supports Perfect Forward Secrecy
C: It is required whenever certificates are used
D: It is less secure and should be used only when the third-party device does not support Main Mode
Vysvetleni: Main Mode is the preferred and default mode. It is more secure, partially encrypted and uses six packets. Aggressive Mode is less secure, uses three packets and should be used only when a third-party device does not support Main Mode.

OTAZKA: A security engineer enables Perfect Forward Secrecy in the VPN community. During which IKEv1 phase does the optional additional Diffie-Hellman exchange occur?
A: Phase II, depending on PFS configuration
B: Phase I only
C: During SmartEvent correlation
D: During Management HA manual synchronization
Vysvetleni: In IKEv1 Phase II, an optional Diffie-Hellman key exchange can occur based on the Perfect Forward Secrecy option. Phase I always includes Diffie-Hellman key creation and exchange for the IKE communication itself.

OTAZKA: Which statement correctly describes IKEv1 Phase II Quick Mode?
A: It is used only to authenticate administrators to SmartConsole
B: It creates one bidirectional IKE SA and always lasts one day
C: It negotiates protection for network traffic between VPN Domains and results in two unidirectional IPSec SAs
D: It replaces Main Mode and Aggressive Mode in IKEv2
Vysvetleni: IKEv1 Phase II prepares encryption parameters for the actual VPN Domain traffic. It is called Quick Mode, uses three packets, and results in two unidirectional IPSec SAs with a default validity of one hour.

OTAZKA: A VPN design uses internally managed Check Point Gateways. An administrator proposes using a Pre-Shared Secret between them. What is wrong with this design?
A: Internally managed VPN Gateways require Aggressive Mode
B: Pre-Shared Secret is mandatory for internally managed VPN Gateways
C: Internally managed VPN Gateways cannot use certificates
D: Pre-Shared Secret cannot be used with internally managed VPN Gateways
Vysvetleni: The course states that PSK cannot be used in the case of internally managed VPN Gateways. Internally managed Gateways are always authenticated using certificates from the same Internal Certificate Authority.

OTAZKA: Externally managed VPN Gateways or Interoperable Devices are introduced into a VPN design. What additional certificate-related work may be required compared with internally managed Gateways?
A: Explicitly establish trust so each Gateway trusts and validates the issuer of the peer certificate
B: Disable certificates and force LDAP Group authentication
C: Convert all VPN Gateways to Dynamic Objects
D: Run cpstop and cpstart on the Management Server
Vysvetleni: Internally managed Gateways receive certificates from the same ICA and no extra action is required. With externally managed Gateways or Interoperable Devices, trust must be explicitly established so each peer can validate the certificate issuer.

OTAZKA: A VPN architect says that a VPN Community is only a visual group in SmartConsole and has no tunnel parameters. Which correction is best?
A: A VPN Community is only a SmartEvent report category
B: A VPN Community is a named collection of VPN Domains and protected Gateways, and tunnel attributes are defined in the community
C: A VPN Community is a NAT object used to create Proxy ARP automatically
D: A VPN Community is a Package Repository for VPN hotfixes
Vysvetleni: A VPN Community is not just cosmetic. It groups VPN Domains protected by VPN Gateways and defines the tunnel attributes such as encryption and authentication parameters.

OTAZKA: In a Star VPN Community, when can two Center Gateways establish VPN tunnels with each other?
A: Only when both belong to the Center Group and the Mesh Center Gateways option is enabled
B: Always, because any two members of a Star community are automatically fully meshed
C: Never, because Star communities allow only Center to Satellite tunnels
D: Only if both use Pre-Shared Secret authentication
Vysvetleni: In a Star topology, tunnels are established between a Center and a Satellite. Center-to-Center tunnels are possible only when both Gateways are in the Center Group and the Mesh Center Gateways option is enabled.

OTAZKA: A-GW is in three VPN communities. B-GW is already paired with A-GW in one Mesh community. Why would adding B-GW to another Mesh community that also contains A-GW be invalid?
A: Because the same Gateway pair would match more than one VPN Community
B: Because a Gateway can never be in more than one VPN Community
C: Because Mesh communities cannot include internally managed Gateways
D: Because PSK is required for every Mesh member
Vysvetleni: A Gateway can be a member of more than one VPN Community, but the same pair of Gateways must not be paired in more than one community. If A-GW and B-GW match two communities, the configuration is invalid.

OTAZKA: Two Satellite Gateways in a Star Community need to communicate with each other. What does the course call the design where their traffic is routed through the Center Gateway tunnels?
A: Central Deployment
B: Policy Installation Flow
C: Management HA Collision
D: VPN Routing
Vysvetleni: Satellite-to-Satellite tunnels are not directly established in a Star community. If required, traffic can be routed through the Center Gateway using VPN Routing options.

OTAZKA: A Firewall rule has VPN set to a specific VPN Community but leaves Source and Destination as Any. What is the important effect of the VPN Community cell?
A: The rule applies only to SmartEvent reports
B: The rule becomes an implied Any Any Accept for all Internet traffic
C: The rule disables encryption because Source and Destination are Any
D: The rule is still limited to the VPN Domains defined in that community
Vysvetleni: Including the VPN Community in the Firewall rule automatically limits the relevant traffic to the VPN Domains defined in that community. Source and Destination can further restrict hosts or direction, but Any does not mean all non-VPN traffic when the VPN community is specified.

OTAZKA: Which statement best separates the responsibilities of Firewall policy and VPN community behavior?
A: The VPN community decides which traffic is tagged as VPN traffic and encryption parameters, while Firewall policy decides whether packets are accepted or dropped
B: The Firewall policy creates IKE SAs, while VPN only logs accepted packets
C: The VPN community installs Access Control policy, while Firewall policy only defines certificates
D: Both are identical and the VPN column has no enforcement meaning
Vysvetleni: The course distinguishes the VPN and Firewall roles. VPN handles encryption, decryption and encapsulation, and the community controls VPN tagging and tunnel parameters. The Firewall policy decides allow or drop.

OTAZKA: SmartEvent is needed for executive dashboards, event investigation and scheduled reports. Where can the SmartEvent Blade be deployed according to the course?
A: Only on a Security Gateway Cluster member
B: Only on the Primary Management Server, never on a dedicated server
C: Integrated with the Security Management Server architecture, optionally on a dedicated Log or SmartEvent Server
D: Only inside the Compliance Dashboard
Vysvetleni: SmartEvent is integrated with the Security Management Server architecture. It can also be deployed on a dedicated Log or SmartEvent Server, which is useful in larger or log-heavy environments.

OTAZKA: An administrator changes SmartEvent Event Policy settings but notices that the new behavior is not applied. What step is most likely missing?
A: Install the Event Policy
B: Run migrate_server verify
C: Enable Merge manual proxy ARP configuration
D: Run vpn tu option 0
Vysvetleni: SmartEvent Event Policy changes must be installed before they are applied. This is separate from installing Access Control policy on Gateways, but the concept is similar. Changing the GUI setting alone is not enough.

OTAZKA: What is the purpose of a SmartEvent Correlation Unit in General Settings?
A: It performs NAT address translation for Management Server control connections
B: It stores CPUSE packages for Central Deployment
C: It analyzes log entries and identifies events
D: It synchronizes Management HA peers
Vysvetleni: The Correlation Unit is added with its respective Log Servers and analyzes log entries to identify events. It is a SmartEvent processing component, not an upgrade, NAT or Management HA component.

OTAZKA: A team wants SmartEvent to analyze previously generated logs from a log file rather than only current incoming logs. Which General Settings feature is designed for this?
A: Network Feeds
B: Offline Jobs
C: Dynamic Objects
D: VPN Routing
Vysvetleni: Offline Jobs configure an Offline Log File process. Each Offline Job is associated with a Log Server and a log file from which previously generated logs are taken.

OTAZKA: Why is defining the Internal Network important in SmartEvent General Settings?
A: It selects the Package Repository source for Central Deployment
B: It automatically creates Manual NAT rules for all internal networks
C: It forces all Gateways to use IKEv2
D: It helps SmartEvent determine whether events originated internally or externally
Vysvetleni: The Internal Network setting helps SmartEvent understand event direction and origin, such as whether traffic or behavior originated inside or outside the organization. This improves event interpretation and reporting.

OTAZKA: Which list contains valid examples of SmartEvent Automatic Reactions from the course?
A: Export the Management database, calculate md5sum, and import logs
B: Create a Secondary Management Server, run First Time Configuration Wizard, and install licenses
C: Send mail, block source or event activity, run an external script, or generate an SNMP Trap
D: Create Proxy ARP, enable NAT, and configure VPN Domains
Vysvetleni: Automatic Reactions are responses triggered when events are detected. The course examples include mail, blocking a source or event activity, running an external script and generating an SNMP Trap.

OTAZKA: A SmartEvent administrator wonders why some Network Objects exist in SmartEvent even though they were not manually created there. What is the reason?
A: Network objects are downloaded from Check Point Cloud as Updatable Objects
B: Network objects are copied from the Management Server during the initial sync and updated at a set interval
C: Network objects are created only by VPN tunnel negotiation
D: Network objects are created by CPUSE verification
Vysvetleni: SmartEvent receives Network Objects from the Management Server during initial synchronization and updates them periodically. Additional objects can still be added when necessary or useful.

OTAZKA: What does the Compliance Software Blade continuously examine using Continuous Compliance Monitoring?
A: Only Office 365 Updatable Objects against Check Point Cloud
B: Only administrator audit logs against VPN communities
C: Security Gateways, Software Blades, policies and configuration settings against regulatory standards and security best practices
D: Only Gateway CPU usage against SNMP thresholds
Vysvetleni: Compliance uses Continuous Compliance Monitoring to examine Gateways, Software Blades, policies and configuration settings against regulatory standards and best practices, then suggests corrective measures.

OTAZKA: Which set correctly describes the five main Compliance Dashboard widgets?
A: Security Best Practices, Gateways and Servers, Blades, Action Items and Messages, Regulatory Compliance
B: VPN Communities, IKE SAs, IPSec SAs, Shared Secrets, Tunnel Management
C: CPUSE, CDT, Package Repository, SmartUpdate, Deployment Agent
D: Primary, Secondary, Collision, Synchronization, Fetch Policy
Vysvetleni: The Compliance Dashboard contains five customizable views or widgets named Security Best Practices, Gateways and Servers, Blades, Action Items and Messages, and Regulatory Compliance.

OTAZKA: A Compliance Best Practice shows a score. How are Best Practices scored according to the course note?
A: By the number of VPN tunnels currently established
B: Only as Critical, High, Medium and Low based on CVSS
C: Only as Pass or Fail with no numeric scores
D: On a 0 to 100 scale by averaging results for each examined object, although some tests are only compliant or non-compliant
Vysvetleni: Best Practices are scored on a 0 to 100 scale by averaging results for each examined object. The course also notes that some best practices are scored only as compliant or non-compliant.

OTAZKA: A Compliance Best Practice test finds a deficiency and creates an Action Item. Which lifecycle behavior is correct?
A: The Action Item is stored only in SmartEvent Offline Jobs
B: The Action Item automatically changes the policy without administrator review
C: The Action Item expires after one hour even if not corrected
D: The Action Item has a helpful corrective description, no due date by default, and is deleted after the next scan if the corrective steps are completed
Vysvetleni: When a deficiency is found, Compliance creates an Action Item describing corrective measures. Due dates are not assigned automatically. After corrective steps are completed, the Compliance Blade deletes the Action Item after the next scan.

OTAZKA: Which statement correctly compares Global Compliance tests and Object-based Compliance tests?
A: Global tests require IKEv2, while object-based tests require IKEv1
B: Global tests are SmartEvent reports, while object-based tests are VPN tunnels
C: Global tests run only during CPUSE upgrades, while object-based tests run only after cpstop
D: Global tests examine settings for the entire organization, while object-based tests examine particular objects such as Gateways and profiles
Vysvetleni: The course defines two types of Compliance Best Practice tests. Global tests examine configuration settings for the entire organization. Object-based tests examine configuration settings for specific objects such as Gateways and profiles.

OTAZKA: When are automatic Continuous Compliance Monitoring scans performed?
A: Daily and after SmartConsole changes affecting Gateway or policy configuration are published
B: Only after every Security Gateway reboot
C: Only when a VPN tunnel is deleted with vpn tu
D: Only after a Management database export
Vysvetleni: CCM performs automatic scans once daily to find changes made through CLI or scripts, and after an administrator publishes SmartConsole changes that affect Gateway or policy configuration. Manual rescans can also be run from Compliance settings.

OTAZKA: An administrator wants to manually rescan Compliance after changing several settings. Where is this initiated?
A: Global Properties, NAT, Merge manual proxy ARP configuration
B: GATEWAYS & SERVERS, Fetch Policy, Add
C: LOGS & EVENTS, SmartEvent Policy, vpn tu
D: MANAGE & SETTINGS, Blades, Compliance Settings, Engine Status, Rescan
Vysvetleni: Manual Compliance rescan is initiated from MANAGE & SETTINGS, then Blades, then Compliance Settings, where the Engine Status section contains Rescan. This is different from refreshing the dashboard view.

OTAZKA: Which upgrade method is described as an in-place upgrade for all Gaia machine types?
A: Central Deployment Tool only
B: CPUSE using the Deployment Agent
C: migrate_server import only
D: SmartEvent Offline Job
Vysvetleni: CPUSE is the in-place upgrade mechanism handled by the Deployment Agent on Gaia OS. It supports Hotfixes, Jumbo Hotfix Accumulators and Major Versions and can be used through Gaia Portal or Gaia Clish.

OTAZKA: Why should the CPUSE Deployment Agent be updated before installing a Hotfix or major version upgrade?
A: It enables Management HA collision recovery
B: It creates VPN communities automatically
C: Deployment Agent updates allow Gaia to use the latest upgrade packages and handle package-specific prerequisites
D: It imports SmartEvent reports into the database
Vysvetleni: The course states that the Deployment Agent must be kept up to date. Updates let Gaia use the latest upgrade packages and are required by certain software packages to handle specific prerequisites.

OTAZKA: A Gateway has no Internet connectivity and an administrator wants to use CPUSE with an offline package. Which order is correct?
A: Import the offline package first, then verify it before installation
B: Verify the package first, then import it only if verification fails
C: Install the package first, then verify after reboot
D: Run migrate_server export because CPUSE cannot use offline packages
Vysvetleni: For offline packages, the package must be imported before it can be verified. Verification checks whether the package can be installed and what installation types are available.

OTAZKA: During an in-place CPUSE upgrade of a Security Management Server, what extra behavior occurs after files are installed and the system reboots?
A: All VPN SAs are converted to permanent tunnels
B: The management database is converted and imported to the new version
C: Proxy ARP is generated for all NAT rules
D: Every Secondary Management Server becomes Active
Vysvetleni: A Management Server CPUSE upgrade continues after reboot by converting and importing the database to the new version. This is not the same as a Gateway upgrade. SmartConsole must also be updated to the new version.

OTAZKA: In the CPUSE workflow for upgrading a Management, Log or SmartEvent Server, why is a JSON configuration file sometimes needed?
A: It is required for every CPUSE upgrade regardless of IP changes
B: It is required if an IP address changes on any Management, Log or SmartEvent Server in the environment
C: It stores the VPN shared secret for third-party peers
D: It defines the Package Repository source as Automatic
Vysvetleni: The CPUSE high-level workflow notes that a JSON configuration file is required when there is an IP change on any Management, Log or SmartEvent Server in the environment. Without an IP change, this specific step may not apply.

OTAZKA: Which method is identified as the best-practice remote upgrade method for Gateways and Clusters from SmartConsole?
A: SmartEvent scheduled report
B: Manual migrate_server import
C: Central Deployment with SmartConsole
D: Management HA manual sync
Vysvetleni: Central Deployment with SmartConsole is the remote upgrade method and is described as the best practice for Gateways and Clusters. CPUSE is an in-place upgrade mechanism, while CDT is a separate automation utility run from Expert mode.

OTAZKA: A large environment uses Central Deployment from SmartConsole. What concurrency and queue behavior should the administrator expect?
A: All selected Gateways install simultaneously without limit
B: Up to 10 Gateways can install at the same time, with up to 30 selected and queued
C: Only one Gateway can be selected and no queue exists
D: Exactly 30 Gateways install at the same time
Vysvetleni: Central Deployment can run installations on up to 10 Gateways at the same time. A total batch of 30 Gateways can be selected and queued, and the Management Server starts another Gateway when one completes.

OTAZKA: Which prerequisite list best fits Central Deployment with SmartConsole?
A: Only SmartEvent enabled and a Compliance score above 80
B: Administrator write permission on SmartUpdate, latest CPUSE Deployment Agent on the Gateway, working SIC, installed policy, and Internet connectivity if using Check Point Cloud
C: Only VPN Routing enabled and Aggressive Mode disabled
D: Only a Secondary Management Server in Standby mode
Vysvetleni: Central Deployment requires appropriate SmartUpdate permissions, an updated CPUSE Deployment Agent, working SIC between Management and Gateway, a policy installed on the Gateway, and Internet connectivity on Management and Gateway if deploying through Check Point Cloud.

OTAZKA: When selecting package location source in Central Deployment, what does Automatic mean?
A: Always use the Management database export file
B: Always download only from the Gateway and ignore the repository
C: Always upload from the administrator workstation directly to each Gateway
D: Use the repository if the package is available, otherwise use Check Point Cloud
Vysvetleni: The package location source options are Automatic, Gateway and Management. Automatic uses the Package Repository if the package is available, otherwise it uses Check Point Cloud. Gateway downloads from Cloud and Management uses the Package Repository.

OTAZKA: After installing a Hotfix or Upgrade with Central Deployment, which extra policy action is required if Threat Prevention policy is used?
A: Run Pre-Upgrade Verifier on the Management database
B: Delete all IPSec SAs with vpn tu
C: Install Threat Prevention policy after the installation is completed
D: Create a Network Feed object
Vysvetleni: The course notes that if Threat Prevention policy is used, it must be installed after the Central Deployment installation is completed. This is easy to miss because the software installation itself may have succeeded.

OTAZKA: What is the Central Deployment Tool, CDT, in the course material?
A: An automation utility installed on the Security Management Server and run from Expert mode
B: A SmartConsole view used only for Compliance dashboards
C: A VPN tunnel utility used to list IKE SAs
D: A Gaia Portal wizard for the first ElasticXL member
Vysvetleni: CDT is installed on the Security Management Server and run from Expert mode. It automates upgrades across large environments and can also take snapshots, run scripts, push or pull files and automate RMA backup or restore.

OTAZKA: Which sequence best describes CDT operation at a high level?
A: Approve SmartEvent fingerprint, create an Offline Job, run a Compliance report
B: Export the Management database, create mdss.json, import database, disconnect old server
C: Validate Gateway state, prepare Access Control Policy and Gateway object changes, execute deployment plan including scripts and CPUSE package operations, validate policy and state again
D: Enable IPSec VPN, create a Mesh community, configure shared secret, run vpn tu
Vysvetleni: CDT validates required processes, prepares Access Control Policy for the Gateway by changing version and configuration, executes a deployment plan including pre scripts, CPUSE update and package push or install, validates policy installation, runs post scripts and validates state again.

OTAZKA: Which best-practice upgrade order is emphasized for management and logging components?
A: Upgrade the Primary Security Management Server before dedicated Log or SmartEvent Servers
B: Upgrade all dedicated Log Servers before the Primary Management Server
C: Upgrade Secondary Management Servers first because they are read-only
D: Upgrade only Security Gateways and leave Management on the old release
Vysvetleni: The upgrade guidance says to upgrade Primary Security Management Servers before dedicated Log or SmartEvent Servers. Module 6 adds that the Primary Management Server should be upgraded before a Management HA Server or dedicated Log or SmartEvent Servers.

OTAZKA: When would an Advanced Upgrade with migration be preferred over a simple in-place CPUSE upgrade?
A: When only installing Event Policy
B: When only deleting existing IPSec SAs
C: When only changing a Network Feed update interval
D: When doing a fresh deployment of the new version or replacing the device and manually exporting and importing the Management database
Vysvetleni: CPUSE in-place upgrades automatically handle the database conversion. Advanced Upgrade is used for a fresh installation, new appliance or device replacement where the Management database must be exported and imported manually.

OTAZKA: Before exporting a Management database for an Advanced Upgrade, why must pending SmartConsole changes be published?
A: The export reads directly from unpublished administrator sessions
B: The export includes only the current database revision
C: The export fails if any policy has comments
D: The export includes only unpublished sessions by design
Vysvetleni: The export procedure exports the current database revision. Unpublished changes are not part of that revision, so they must be published before the export if they need to be migrated.

OTAZKA: Which command correctly runs the Pre-Upgrade Verifier for a connected Security Management Server targeting R82?
A: fw fetch localhost
B: ./migrate_server export -v R82 -l -x
C: ./migrate_server verify -v R82
D: show cluster info policy
Vysvetleni: From $FWDIR/scripts in Expert mode, a connected server uses ./migrate_server verify -v R82. If the server is not connected to the Internet, the command adds -skip_upgrade_tools_check.

OTAZKA: A Security Management Server has no Internet access during database export. Which option is added to migrate_server commands to bypass the Upgrade Tools online check?
A: -force_collision_mode
B: -skip_upgrade_tools_check
C: -sync_peer_full
D: -use_gateway_proxy
Vysvetleni: For disconnected systems, the course adds -skip_upgrade_tools_check to the verify, export or import commands as needed. This is not a generic force option, it is specific to the upgrade tools check.

OTAZKA: By default, what is not included in a migrate_server export or import, and what options are used to include them?
A: Objects and policies are not included by default, use -o and -p
B: Logs and log indexes are not included by default, use -l and -x respectively
C: Certificates are not included by default, use -c and -k
D: Administrators are not included by default, use -a and -u
Vysvetleni: The course states that logs and log indexes are not included by default. Use -l to include logs and -x to include indexes. Including them requires more space and increases export and import time.

OTAZKA: After copying an exported .txz database file to a new Management Server, what integrity step is required before import?
A: Install Event Policy from SmartEvent
B: Run vpn tu and delete all IPSec SAs
C: Calculate md5sum on the transferred file and compare it to the hash calculated on the original server
D: Enable Merge manual proxy ARP configuration
Vysvetleni: The course requires calculating the md5sum hash of the export file and comparing it after transfer. It also notes that transfer should be done in binary mode to avoid corruption.

OTAZKA: When is /var/log/mdss.json required during Management database import?
A: When the target Management Server has a different IP address than the source, or any Log or SmartEvent Server in the environment has a different IP
B: For every import, even when all IP addresses remain identical
C: Only when importing logs but not indexes
D: Only when using IKEv2 with IPv6
Vysvetleni: The JSON configuration file is required if the target Security Management Server IP differs from the source, or if any Log or SmartEvent Server IP changes. If no servers changed IP addresses, the special JSON file is not needed.

OTAZKA: What important side effect occurs when running migrate_server import?
A: The command automatically sets every Management HA peer to Active
B: The command automatically restarts Check Point services by running cpstop and cpstart
C: The command automatically deletes old licenses and installs new ones
D: The command automatically creates VPN communities
Vysvetleni: The import command restarts Check Point services automatically with cpstop and cpstart. Administrators must plan for this service impact during migration.

OTAZKA: Which statement correctly describes ElasticXL scale limits in R82 according to the course?
A: ElasticXL supports exactly two Members total and no second site
B: ElasticXL supports unlimited Members per site as long as gClish is enabled
C: ElasticXL is supported only before R82 and replaces SmartEvent
D: ElasticXL requires R82 or higher and supports up to three Members per site with a maximum of two sites
Vysvetleni: ElasticXL Cluster is introduced for Release 82 and higher. The course states it can support three Members per site and up to two sites, for a maximum of six potential Security Gateways.

OTAZKA: What is the Single Management Object, SMO, in an ElasticXL Cluster?
A: A VPN community used for Center and Satellite routing
B: A dedicated Log Server that stores all ElasticXL reports
C: A Secondary Management Server used only for Management HA
D: A Security Gateway object in SmartConsole that provides one management IP for communication and policy installation for the whole ElasticXL Cluster
Vysvetleni: The SMO represents the ElasticXL Cluster in SmartConsole. It provides a single management IP for management communication and policy installation, and defines the security features deployed on the ElasticXL Cluster.

OTAZKA: In an ElasticXL Cluster, how does the Management Server communicate with cluster members for configuration and policy changes?
A: It must communicate separately with every Member before each policy installation
B: It communicates only with the SMO
C: It communicates only with the hidden Sync interface
D: It communicates through the VPN community center Gateway
Vysvetleni: The course says the Management Server communicates only with the SMO for configuration and policy changes. The SMO accepts all traffic and distributes it among other Members on the same site.

OTAZKA: What is gClish used for in an ElasticXL Cluster?
A: Editing SmartEvent Event Policy
B: Running migrate_server export for Management database migration
C: Managing and monitoring the entire ElasticXL Cluster with commands that affect all Members collectively
D: Creating LDAP Group objects
Vysvetleni: Global Clish, or gClish, is a specialized shell for cluster-wide operations. Commands executed there affect all ElasticXL Cluster Members collectively, avoiding the need to connect to each Member individually.

OTAZKA: Which interface mapping is accurate for ElasticXL Cluster Members?
A: External is hidden in SmartConsole and receives a 192.0.2.0/24 address
B: Management is always eth0 without bonding, Sync is visible in SmartConsole, and Internal uses the Management IP
C: There is no dedicated Sync interface because ElasticXL uses only policy installation
D: Management is a bonded interface represented by magg1, Sync is a hidden bonded interface with an automatically assigned 192.0.2.0/24 address, and Internal and External are data interfaces
Vysvetleni: ElasticXL requires at least four interfaces per Member. Management traffic uses the bonded magg1 interface. Synchronization uses a hidden bonded Sync interface with an automatic 192.0.2.0/24 address. Internal and External are data interfaces with unicast MAC addresses.

OTAZKA: During ElasticXL deployment, which appliance must run the First Time Configuration Wizard?
A: Only the first Gateway appliance that becomes the SMO
B: Every new Member before it can be added
C: Only the Security Management Server
D: No appliance because ElasticXL forbids First Time Configuration Wizard
Vysvetleni: ElasticXL simplifies deployment by configuring the first Gateway appliance as the SMO. The First Time Configuration Wizard is run only once and only on the SMO. New Members need Gaia installed and physical connectivity to the same networks, then they can be added from Pending Gateways.

OTAZKA: After the ElasticXL SMO First Time Configuration Wizard completes, why might Internal, External or DMZ interfaces still need work?
A: The wizard intentionally deletes all data interfaces
B: The wizard configures only the Management and Sync interfaces, so other interfaces must be configured afterward
C: The wizard configures only VPN communities
D: The wizard configures only SmartEvent and Compliance blades
Vysvetleni: The course notes that the First Time Configuration Wizard only configures the Management interface and the Sync interface. Other interfaces such as Internal, External and DMZ must be configured after the initial configuration.

OTAZKA: A new ElasticXL appliance is connected to the same networks and powered on. What is the expected way to add it to the cluster?
A: Select it from Pending Gateways on the SMO or Cluster Management and add it to the ElasticXL Cluster
B: Run First Time Configuration Wizard on the new appliance and create a separate ClusterXL object
C: Export the SMO database and import it onto the new appliance manually
D: Create a Manual NAT rule for the new appliance management IP
Vysvetleni: New ElasticXL Members are automatically listed for the SMO when they have Gaia installed and physical connectivity. The administrator adds them through Pending Gateways, after which configuration is replicated and provisioning completes.

OTAZKA: After adding an ElasticXL Member, what happens to software packages, settings and security policies?
A: They are imported through SmartEvent Offline Jobs
B: They must be manually copied with SFTP from each existing Member
C: They automatically clone from the SMO to the new Member
D: They are created only after Management HA manual sync
Vysvetleni: ElasticXL Members automatically clone software packages, settings and security policies from the SMO. This automatic cloning is a major operational difference from traditional per-member configuration workflows.
